title: Settings
subtitle: A dedicated place for console-level preferences, policy controls, and future system configuration.

update.title: Framework Update & Restart
update.subtitle: Run the deployment-owned framework update command. A restart can be requested after the command succeeds.
update.restartPending: A restart request has been recorded. Waiting for process shutdown and relaunch.
update.restartRequestedHint: A restart request is already pending. Reason: {reason}, requested by: {requestedBy}
update.noRestartResult: Framework update command completed without requesting restart.
update.confirmText: The server will run the configured framework update command. The command is maintained by the deployment and may pull code, sync dependencies, or run other deployment steps.
update.confirmWarning: Make sure the command is suitable for this deployment. A failed command will not request restart.

build.title: Dashboard Build
build.subtitle: Run the build command in the local Dashboard source directory and produce the served dist assets.
build.loadFailed: Failed to load Dashboard build state
build.builtResult: Dashboard build completed. Refresh the page to load it.
build.builtToast: Dashboard build completed. Refresh the page to load it
build.confirmText: The server will run the configured build command in the Dashboard source directory.
build.confirmHint: The build may require pnpm/npm dependencies to be installed. Backend restart is not required; refresh the browser after build.

credentials.title: Administrator Account
credentials.subtitle: Update the login username and password.
credentials.mustChangeHint: You are using default credentials. You must update both username and password before continuing.
credentials.passwordMismatch: The new passwords do not match
credentials.defaultNotAllowed: Default credentials are not allowed. Please update both username and password

pricing.title: Pricing Display
pricing.subtitle: Control the currency and token unit used by model pricing inputs and cost displays.
pricing.hint: The model editor follows this unit for display and input, while values are still stored internally per one million tokens so changing units will not damage existing data.
